item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I remember this one! it's the first Series V SCP i saw! and still is as of now, my favorite
Altho yes, it has typos which seems to be it's main problem (and one who it's clearly been woked on!), i genuinely had fun reading it, AND it gave me a good excuse to listen to some good piano music (mainly In The Hall of the Mountain King, fav song) and the idea of a predatory piano is amusingly terrifying

Plus, my first thought was the nightmarish Mario 64 piano so… +1 for that too

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The title was certainly clever. The tone was a bit weak and I didn't really feel all the parts come together that well. Particularly, the rope didn't seem that aesthetically similar to the other aspects of the piano's operation.

Neutral vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: At the end, “all of whom were eaten by SCP-4088,” I think “consumed” would be a better replacement for “eaten” as the description says the prey simply disappears after being put into its interior.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"… first 58 second…"

Oh, well done.

item.title: Ok I like this
item.content:encoded: Mostly because how its billy Joel that sends it into a crazed state. Also its creative and not just a copy of that piano from Mario (which isn’t that scary, the chain chomp is scarier).
Pee-yah-know

item.title: Holy Holys
item.content:encoded: +100! Thank you guys for all your support! This is a huge milestone for me and I’m so happy that you all enjoyed my dumb little piano and bad pun so much.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
When reading an SCP, i'm usually excited to read something, or i'm drawn in my story, or the horror effect brings out the fear factor, or the overall uniqueness peaks my curiosity.

This SCP is probably the first time i've just had FUN reading it, Not "haha" funny fun. Just pure fun. It was a fun read.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I missed milestone +50, so I’ll go with +69. Thanks for all the support on this guys!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
piano pun

Is it a bad thing it took a second to understand that?

Piano can drop on people given it's accompanied rope

Why am I not surprised… (Fun fact: no one has died irl from a falling piano)

Killed 8 people after playing the song "piano man"

LOL. take my +1. TAKE IT!!!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Aaaand, now I'm imagining a running piano playing Toccata and Fugue in D Minor. This entry felt like a nice break from all of the insanity that goes on within just about every other entry. Just your average, carnivorous, interesting piano. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Was this inspired by the piano from Super Mario 64? Because it reminded me of Super Mario 64

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm choosing to believe that the researchers are wrong and projecting re: Flight of the Valkaries.

The piano isn't scared.

It's excited! It's an indestructible anomaly…when it hears the breach alarm it sings with glee! "Fly my brothers fly! be free and exact our dark revenge!"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just a quick aside, you're looking for semantic satiation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Great skip, despite the little typos. I think I had a dream about a piano like this when I still went to music school. Wonder what its mating call would be?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: the name immediately drew me in and it fills my 2 criteria for good skip (made me feel an emotion, written well) so good job i'm gonna give this a plus 1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Really angry classical music.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very creative, well-written, original, and gave me Vietnam flashbacks to the mad piano from Mario 64. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Alright, so I’ve gone through attempting to find some sentences that were clunky and make them smoother. Thanks for the crit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Actually, thanks for combing through this. I was starting to go through it again after I saw the comment above when I refreshed the page and saw your comment. I’ve fixed the sentence mistakes that you’ve pointed out, and I’ve ditched the whole “subject gets transfixed while the piano is falling” because it doesn’t really add much. Again, thanks for the critique so I can make my SCP better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Agree with 'amusing concept, but execution needs work'. In addition to the typos, the phrasing is clunky and weird in a lot of sections.

SCP-4088 doesn't appear to be damaged when it falls from SCP-4088-1, even from extremely high heights.

Like, this would read so much more smoothly if it were just 'SCP-4088 is undamaged on falling, even from extreme heights' or something like that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I will probably regret coming down so hard here…

+ show block
– hide block

…but this is sub-draft-level writing. And I want to like this because I am a fan of classical music, pianos, and Mr. Joel.

In addition to the errors and issues Uncle Nic pointed out…

SCP-4088 is mobile, bending it's legs

its, not it's

SCP-4088-1's anomalous properties only manifest when left in a closed room with SCP-4088 and not observed…The way that this occurs is currently unknown, as this behavior only manifests when no humans are observing the room though any means.

This is inexcusably redundant, you say the exact same thing two sentences apart from one another.

Additional instances of repeated words:

SCP-4088 then will wait wait until

The damage caused to the subject will almost always end in death, with the cause

to regrow the length of its rope through unknown means, at a rate of 2 meters regrown an hour.

Both visually and compositionally, the second paragraph needs to be split into two; it is too long as is.

The installment of the victim's sudden inability to run away, left instead in helplessness to only look up with a scared expression introduces a strikingly powerful psychological/neuro/musculoskeletal component to this anomaly's abilities, yet it is never detailed and is only placed compositionally to ensure that people die. It also doesn't make any sense that it isn't used by the anomaly at other times. You've already mentioned that the piano can move at remarkable speed, why can't it simply drop at similar speeds too?

I'm sorry, I either upvote or novote 98% of the time, but I just have to downvote this.

I do love the imagery of a predatorily-toothed piano.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh man, the idea of being alone in a room with a piano, who is hanging from the ceiling going "Dun Dun Dun Dun Dun Dun Duuuun, Dun Dun Duuuuun, Dun Dun Duuuun" and being told to go under it is terrifying and striking. +1 for the great imagery and concept. I did notice several grammar mistakes, however. The first paragraph has a "Wait wait", which is obviously incorrect. I recommend getting a grammar checker.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Was that pun intentional?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1 for Billy Joel reference, AND reminding me of that godawful piano from super Mario 64

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have to admit, my reaction to this was fairly low-key, though I have to give you a lot of credit for the predation behavior. That's just so out-there, I can't hate this article, but I'm a novote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Some animals roar loudly to scare predators. This one plays angry classical music.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think it’s a bit better as a sneaky predator though. All of the other traits of the SCP make it seem like a creepy predator that hides. Then it pounces on you when you least expect it. It pretending to be weak then showing unseen speed, I feel, fits the SCP more than it trying to gain sympathy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for the feedback. Reading it over again, I can see how it sounded clunky in some places so I’ve tried to smooth that out. I went back and reworded a few sentences and parts of the table. Hope that it’s less choppy now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Think I would've upvoted, if the piano in the end was weak and grateful to be fed and saved from death, rather than sneaky super-powerful predator.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
One of the Series V Skips, posted on the first few days, that will make it. Good job. I don't think I will get the thought of SCP-4088 trying to scare other SCPs out of my mind. I hope I won't.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As much as I enjoy the concept, the clinical tone isn't quite there and there are too many errors to warrant an upvote from me right now.

But for real though, predatory pianos are awesome.

EDIT: I'll clip some bits that stand out the most shortly.

+ Open
- Close

Any music played by SCP-4088 is to be recorded and sent to Head Researcher Zarakolv

Missing a full stop.

SCP-4088 is mobile, moving around

then will lurch towards the prey and attempt to slam its lid shut on the prey. SCP-4088 will continue to do this until the prey is deceased, then it will drop the prey into its interior where the body will disappear.

the word 'prey' is used four times with less than ten words between each use, which creates a semantic sensation.

The way that this occurs is currently unknown, as it only appears to occur when no humans are observing the room though any means

The way that SCP-4088-1 is able to support SCP-4088

The subject that is standing under SCP-4088 when it is falling will not attempt to retreat, and only looks up at SCP-4088 with a terrified expression. SCP-4088 falling onto the subject almost always ends in death,

This appears to be the song SCP-4088 uses the most to draw in prey.

Now that I read this again, there's an awful lot of semantic sensation on top of the tonal issues. It also feels as if the 'amounts played' part of the table doesn't add much as we can infer how often it would be heard to be playing the songs based on its established behaviors as a predator.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nice catch. The original name was going to be 88 Keys to the Gates of Hell, but that sounds too edgy and I like the Billy Joel reference instead.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yea, and writing this gave me an excuse to listen to a lot of Piano Man.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The addendum makes the entire SCP work for me, the pieces there are perfect.

SCP-4088 began attempting to play the song repeatedly, but about 58 seconds into the song would falter and attempt to play the song correctly from the beginning

:( never thought I'd feel bad for a carnivorous piano but here we are

Also nice nod to the 88 keys with the number.

+1

EDIT: Caught a typo in the last row of the addendum:

all of whom were eaten by CP-4088

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Sees name on Series V Mainlist.

Oh, a Billy Joel joke? Hmm.

It's a piano that eats people.

Okay, it's well written.

It's a piano that drops on people.

I laughed. Getting better.

It's a piano that plays Ride of the Valkyries to scare off other SCPS

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Required Listening

I’ve been sitting on this one for a long time. Thanks to Gabriel Jade for critiquing this so long ago.

Thanks to Bill-et-al for this spoopy Inktober fan art.

Other Articles of Mine
